free_text: A study of the states of Si-30 with the Si-28(t,p)Si-30
and Si-29(d,p)Si-30 reactions.
Multi-angle magnetic spectrograph
.50 mum Ilford K-1 emulsions.
.DWBA analysis.
.Proton energy spectrum Si-28(t,p)Si-30 and
Si-29(d,p)Si-30 reactions were observed.
By CAJAD.
.Digitizing error of angle.
.Digitizing error of data.
SB
fig 2,3
Self-supporting targets of SiO(2) were used.
The isotopic enrichment is 95% of Si-28.
6 MV Van de Graaff accelerator.
*By authors*.In reaction the thickness of the
target was not known with sufficient accuracy to permit
calculation of reliable values of absolute cross
section.
.The errors are mainly statistical, but include,
where necessary, an allowance for unfolding from other
nearby groups.

fig 5
An enriched silicon target, Si-29 92.0%, Si-28 7.74%
and Si-30 0.26%, the areal density of Si-29 in the
target was deduced to be 20+-4 mug/cm**2.
EN tandem Van de Graaff accelerator.
for 2 MeV deuterons the reaction products were
detected with silicon surface barrier detector.
The outgoing protons were detected with
semiconductor counter telescopes at lab angles of 27.5,
35.0 and 42.5 degrees.
*By authors*.The supplementary measurements were made
in a 50 cm diameter scattering chamber.
Yields of several clearly defined proton groups from
the magnetic spectrograph work were compared with the
data obtained using semiconductor detector and the
target of known thickness.The (d,p) angular
distribution obtained with the magnetic spectrograph
could then be expressed in absolute cross section units
with an accuracy of approximately 25%. Because a beam
of tritons was no longer available it was not possible
to use the above technique to determine an absolute
cross section scale for the (t,p) reaction.
.The error is reported by authors on fig.
.The uncertainty in target thickness.
